steps.title: Set up the compound inequality from the given information
steps.content:
The panda's mass is between $\frac{1}{950}$ and $\frac{1}{850}$ times its mother's mass. Since mother's mass is 95,950 grams, the panda's mass $p$ must satisfy:
$$\frac{95,950}{950} \leq p \leq \frac{95,950}{850}$$

steps.title: Calculate the upper bound and match to the answer
steps.content:
Compute $\frac{95,950}{850}$:
$$850 \times 112 = 95,200, \quad 95,950 - 95,200 = 750$$
$$\frac{95,950}{850} = 112 + \frac{750}{850} \approx 112.88$$
The panda's mass range is $101 \leq p \leq 112.88$. The answer choices display lower bounds, and the correct lower bound is 101. That matches choice B — B is correct.
